completed: conditional restricted row0 marked-polarized cycle-spread certificate
one_page_summary.terminal_question: Does the literature contain, or can an expert construct, a noncircular K3^[n]-type LLV-complement Hodge-class witness outside the certified SH/row0 tautological transport span, with algebraic-cycle realization independent of HodgeConjecture_general?

expert_questions.question: Is there a known explicit K3^[n]-type LLV-complement Hodge class outside SH(X,Q) for the relevant range that is proven algebraic without assuming HodgeConjecture_general?
Is there a known low-n classification, especially for K3^[2], separating tautological/row0 classes from non-tautological LLV-complement classes with algebraic-cycle realizations?
Can Markman monodromy/deformation transport preserve a non-tautological algebraic witness without collapsing the argument into HodgeConjecture_general?
Are Bergeron-Li, Lehn-Sorger, Nakajima, Oberdieck, or related sources sufficient for the needed noncircular witness, or do they only support the certified tautological/row0 chain?
Is the terminal blocker equivalent in difficulty to a known open Hodge/Tate/Mumford-Tate subproblem?

requested_external_input.primary: explicit noncircular K3^[n] LLV-complement algebraic defect witness
requested_external_input.secondary: external theorem proving LLV-complement highest-weight algebraicity in the required range
requested_external_input.fallback: expert confirmation that no known noncircular route exists
